free_text: New experimental study of low-energy (p,g) resonances
in magnesium isotopes.
Institute of Experimental.physics and
Fakult. of physics and astronomy,  Ruhr-Universit.
at Bochum, D-44780 Bochum, Germany
BGO detection
system.
The 400 kV LUNA accelerator.
*By authors*.The effective energy E was determined from
the centroids of the gamma lines for capture to the
ground state and to the 6792 keV state and leads to
2.0-2.4% uncertainty.
S.B
A natural Mg target has been produced by evaporation of
metallic magnesium of natural isotopic composition on
a Ta-backing.The Mg target on Ta has been cut into two
pieces for the HPGe detector and the BGO crystal
measurement, respectively
.A HPGe detector (11.5% relative efficiency,
resolution=2.1 keV at E(g)=1.3 MeV) was placed on
another movable carriage oriented at 55 degree with
respect to the beam axis.
.At HPGe measurements-The uncertainty takes
into account the statistical error and a 3.5% error for
the efficiency.
At BGO measurements -The uncertainty takes into account
the background correction, decay scheme uncertainties
and an error for the simulation.
Weighted mean-Common uncertainties- for stopping power
and charge integration are added quadratically.

metallic magnesium of natural isotopic composition on
a Ta-backing.The Mg target on Ta has been cut into two

measurement, respectively
.A HPGe detector (11.5% relative efficiency,
resolution=2.1 keV at E(g)=1.3 MeV) was placed on
another movable carriage oriented at 55 degree with
respect to the beam axis.
.The gamma-rays from the reaction to be studied
were detected in BGO detection system.
The BGO crystal is a cylinder (length = 28 cm) with a
coaxial hole (diameter = 6 cm) and a radial thickness
of 7 cm.

*By authors*.Numerator-AMS measurement-pointer 2,
Denominator-BGO measurement-pointer 1,
*By authors*.A high-quality AMS measurement is possible
and no systematic difference exists between AMS result
and gamma-ray data.
A natural Mg target has been produced by evaporation of
metallic magnesium of natural isotopic composition on
a Ta-backing.The Mg target on Ta has been cut into two
pieces for the HPGe detector and the BGO crystal
measurement, respectively
The Mg-25 targets for these AMS measurements were
prepared by reducing enriched MgO mixed with zirconium
powder and evaporation with an electron gun. The target
thickness was between 40 and 60 mug/cm**2 corresponding
to an energy loss of 20 and 30 keV at the resonance
energy.
.The AMS measurement has an uncertainty of 2%
arising from the normalization to the reference samples
Additional systematic errors take into account the
uncertainties of the carrier weight-0.2% and the
efficiency of the chemical treatment of the samples-1%.
This leads to an overall systematic uncertainty of 2.5%
for the AMS measurement.
The uncertainties of the corresponding gamma-ray
measurement are given by the absolute efficiency
determination for the BGO detector-3%,
the decay scheme-3%, the ground-state probability
f(0)-1%: a total systematic error of 4%. The
uncertainty of the effective stopping power and the
charge integration represent common uncertainties to
both methods and therefore, were not taken into account
in the error estimate for the wg ratio.
